pr.title: Add comments related to Pikachu's Up B
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added explanatory comments and a few readability macros around Pikachu's Up B / Quick Attack logic, especially stick magnitude handling, zip duration, stored first-zip stick direction, second-zip checks, velocity calculation, and attribute meanings. The PR did not broadly rename symbols despite the author asking for guidance on whether to start renaming; evidence shows mainly comments plus constants replacing repeated literals.
what_changed: Annotated several fields in ftPikachuAttributes with inferred Up B meanings, including x60 as zip duration, x68 and x78 as angle offsets, x8C as minimum stick magnitude, x90/x94 as stick-magnitude-to-velocity slope/intercept, x98 as second zip velocity decay, and xA8 as the minimum stick angle difference between two Up B zips.

Introduced named macros HALF_PI, DEG_TO_RAD, and MAX_STICK_MAG usage in ftpikachu4.c; DEG_TO_RAD replaced repeated 0.017453292f angle conversion literals and MAX_STICK_MAG replaced repeated 0.999f stick cap literals.
Added comments describing ftPikachu_UpdateVel_80125D80 as pointing velocity toward facing direction.

Added comments to grounded Up B zip logic in ftPikachu_80126C0C, including stick magnitude calculation, cap to MAX_STICK_MAG, ground-normal angle check, storage of first zip stick angle, zip duration setup, jump consumption, velocity slope/intercept calculation, facing-direction flip, and second-zip decay.

Added comments to aerial Up B zip logic in ftPikachu_80126E1C, including atan2-derived zip angle, default no-stick case using 90 degrees and max stick magnitude, stored stick input, zip duration, jump consumption, velocity computation with cosf/sinf, and second-zip decay.

Added comments to ftPikachu_80127064 identifying it as likely checking whether a second Up B zip should occur, with notes on stick magnitude threshold and angular difference between current stick and stored first zip direction.
Kept function and field names mostly unchanged; the PR body explicitly notes uncertainty about whether to start renaming symbols.
smart_moves: Used comments to document inferred behavior without overcommitting to permanent symbol names, which is appropriate when author confidence or project naming guidance is uncertain.
Replaced magic angle-conversion and stick-cap literals with macros, improving searchability and making repeated matching-sensitive constants easier to reason about.
Preserved suspicious stack-filling inline helper behavior while swapping in MAX_STICK_MAG, avoiding a risky cleanup that might alter matching characteristics.

Documented dataflow through state variables: first zip stick input is stored in x234C_pos.y/z, x2344_stateVar2_s32 receives zip duration, x2348_stateVar3_s32 gates second-zip behavior, and jumpsUsed is set to MaxJumps.
Separated grounded and aerial Up B commentary, making the shared mechanics visible while preserving differences in ground velocity versus self velocity.
decomp_lessons: When symbol names are uncertain, inline comments on anonymous attributes can capture strong behavioral hypotheses without forcing premature renames.
Repeated float literals such as 0.017453292f and 0.999f are good candidates for local macros when they clearly represent common concepts like degrees-to-radians and maximum analog stick magnitude.

For fighter move decomp, comments that explain state-variable roles are especially useful because fields like x2344_stateVar2_s32, x2348_stateVar3_s32, and x234C_pos can be reused across action callbacks in non-obvious ways.
Keep matching-sensitive stack-fill helper patterns intact even if they look artificial; readability can be improved around them without deleting them.

Attribute comments should describe observed formulas and thresholds, such as velocity = slope * stick_mag + intercept, rather than inventing canonical names not yet supported by review or broader project conventions.
Documenting input-vector angle comparisons helps future work distinguish gameplay logic from collision response and action-transition logic.
naming_conventions: Macros used all-caps descriptive names: HALF_PI, DEG_TO_RAD, MAX_STICK_MAG.
No broad function renaming was performed; existing decomp placeholder-style names such as ftPikachu_80126C0C and ftPikachu_80127064 were retained.
Attribute fields retained offset names like x60, x90, x94, and xA8 with trailing comments instead of being renamed in the struct.
Comments used gameplay terms such as up b zip, second zip, zip duration, zip slope, zip intercept, and second_zip_decay to describe inferred meanings.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved inline helper functions get_max_and_fill_stack and return_and_fill_stack, including stack writes that appear to serve matching constraints.
Changed magic constants to macros without changing their float values: 0.017453292f became DEG_TO_RAD and 0.999f became MAX_STICK_MAG.
Kept formulas structurally intact for stick magnitude sqrtf, angle comparisons via lbvector_AngleXY, velocity calculation through cosf/sinf, and second-zip velocity scaling.
Did not remove redundant-looking casts, temporary variables, or action state transition calls, suggesting the PR was scoped to comments/readability rather than rematching or cleanup.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit process note is from the author: they were unsure whether to start renaming symbols and were looking for general guidance.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftPikachu_80126C0C, ftPikachu_80126E1C, ftPikachu_80127064, or the commented ftPikachuAttributes fields.
Check whether project conventions later prefer struct field renames over offset-field comments for fighter special attributes.
Search for other fighters' special-move decomp PRs that use MAX_STICK_MAG or DEG_TO_RAD style macros.
Verify whether comments such as x68/x78 angle offsets and xA8 minimum stick angle difference were later confirmed or corrected.
Compare with later matching work to see whether get_max_and_fill_stack and return_and_fill_stack remained necessary.
